source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An object is placed 10 cm in front of a convering lens of focal length 10 cm . A diverging lens of focal length -15 cm is placed 5 cm behind the converging lens (Fig. 1.27). Find the position of the final image.
extraction_notes: image field contains PIL image object representations rather than actual file paths or URLs

issues: metadata.image_paths contains PIL image object representations rather than actual file paths or URLs

image_dependency: question_text explicitly references 'Fig. 1.27', indicating some intended figure use
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false in the sample fields
the full numerical setup (object distance, both focal lengths, and lens separation) is already stated in text, so the image may help but does not appear strictly necessary
multi_step: the problem involves a two-lens optical system, which typically requires solving for the first lens image and then using it as input for the second lens
the text provides multiple quantitative conditions that must be combined: object distance, converging-lens focal length, diverging-lens focal length, and 5 cm separation
answer_type is short_text with no choices, so the solver likely needs a worked reasoning chain rather than recognition from options
verifiability: answer_text is a concrete signed numeric result with unit ('$-15 \\mathrm{cm}$'), which is straightforward to check against lens-formula calculations
the physics setup is standard and process checking is feasible from the stated quantities
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true, and both question and answer are present in the registry

metadata.reasoning_chain: For the first lens, the object distance is $u_{1}=10 \text{ cm}$. As $f_{1}=10 \text{ cm}$ also, the image distance is $v_{1}=\text{∞}$. Then for the second lens, the object distance is $u_{2}=\text{∞}$. As $f_{2}=-15 \text{ cm}, v_{2}=-15 \text{ cm}$. Hence, the image coincides with the object.
